parentQuestion: A 28-year-old woman is involved in a motorcycle accident and suffers a spinal co...

scaffolds.q: The patellar (knee jerk) reflex tests the L2-L4 spinal segments.
scaffolds.explain: Yes — and let me show you the elegance of this. When you tap the patellar tendon, you're testing the integrity of the L2-L4 spinal cord segments and the femoral nerve. Stretch receptors in the quadriceps → sensory neurons → L2-L4 dorsal horn → motor neurons → femoral nerve → quadriceps contracts. Simple. Surgical. Diagnostic. Walk through this:.

scaffolds.q: The ankle jerk reflex tests the S1-S2 spinal segments.
scaffolds.explain: Absolutely — and here's where the anatomy becomes a map. The ankle jerk (Achilles reflex) tests S1-S2 via the tibial nerve. Tap the Achilles tendon → gastrocnemius/soleus contracts → foot plantar flexes. If this reflex is intact while the knee jerk is absent, you know the lesion is ABOVE S1 but involves L2-L4. Absorb this:the precision.
